name: Lincoln Financial Field
tagline: Six matches in the loudest open-air bowl in American sports, capped by a July 4 knockout in the nation's founding city.

why.title: Bird Gang Energy
why.text: Philly fans are the loudest, most passionate crowd in American sports, and the stadium's wing-like canopies amplify every roar into a wall of noise.

why.title: July 4 Knockout
why.text: The Round of 16 lands on US Independence Day during America's 250th birthday, in the very city where the country was founded. A once-ever overlap.

why.title: Big Names Coming
why.text: Brazil, France and Croatia all play group games here, so the marquee talent rolls through South Philly.

why.title: Stadium Cluster
why.text: Four pro venues sit side by side, so the whole district turns into one massive matchday block party.

gettingThere: Skip the car and take the SEPTA Broad Street Line subway straight to NRG Station, the southern terminus, then walk five to ten minutes across the lots to the gates. Philadelphia International Airport is only a 10-15 minute drive away, one of the closest airport-to-stadium pairings of the whole tournament.
transit: Broad Street Line subway south to NRG Station, regular fare $2.90, about 15-20 minutes from Center City.
World Cup bonus: free SEPTA rides home from NRG for all six matches, starting at halftime and running two hours after.
Sports Express trains run roughly every 10 minutes on match days with extended late-night service.
Driving means $40-60 parking plus heavy post-match gridlock, so transit is faster.
matchday: The Linc is an open-air bowl with no roof, anchoring the South Philadelphia Sports Complex alongside three other pro arenas. Tailgating in the lots is legendary, so arrive a couple hours early to soak in the pre-match scene.
food: Chickie's & Pete's steps from the complex, the area's premier sports bar famous for its Crabfries.
South Philly cheesesteaks: the classic Pat's King of Steaks vs Geno's Steaks rivalry, both nearby.
East Passyunk Avenue and the Italian Market for a deeper food crawl beyond the stadium.
Stadium is fully cashless, so bring a card or your phone for concessions.
tips: Take the subway: it beats traffic, drops you near the gates, and your ride home is free from halftime onward.
Arrive early for World Cup security; walk-through magnetometer lines run longer than normal NFL game days.
For afternoon matches (Jun 22, 25, 27 and Jul 4), grab upper-deck sideline seats under the canopy for shade; lower bowl and end zones bake in full sun.
Want the loudest atmosphere? Head to the end-zone supporter sections; for sightlines, the lower-bowl sidelines win.
No ticket? The free FIFA Fan Festival runs at Lemon Hill in East Fairmount Park every single day of the tournament; register online for your e-ticket.
Clear bag policy only: bags up to 12 by 6 by 12 inches or a small clutch are allowed.
weather: Expect hot, humid mid-Atlantic summer with highs in the low 80s in June and mid 80s in July (heat index can hit ~97F), plus brief afternoon storms, so pack sunscreen, a hat and water for the unshaded, roofless bowl.
lore: Home of the Philadelphia Eagles, who won Super Bowl LIX after the 2024 season.
Passed its World Cup audition by hosting seven Club World Cup matches in June 2025, with FIFA's turf experts praising the natural-grass HERO Hybrid pitch.
One of the greenest stadiums anywhere: ~11,000 solar panels, 14 wind turbines and LEED Gold certification, running on 100% clean energy.
Hosted WrestleMania 40 in 2024, drawing a record 145,298 fans across two nights.

transport.summary: The Linc sits at the end of SEPTA's Broad Street Line in the South Philadelphia Sports Complex, so transit is by far the easiest way in. SEPTA expects roughly 31,000 riders per match and can move about 15,000 people per hour on the subway with extra World Cup service.

rail.name: Broad Street Line (Metro B subway)
rail.detail: Take the B southbound to NRG, the southern terminus at Broad & Pattison, then walk 5-10 minutes across the lots to the gates; about 15-20 minutes from Center City. SEPTA adds 10 extra B trains to NRG before each match plus added post-game trains.

points.t: across the lots to the gates

rail.deal: Free ride home from NRG: complimentary northbound B service starts at halftime and runs until two hours after the final whistle, sponsored by Airbnb. Rides TO the game are the normal $2.90.

rail.name: Market-Frankford Line (Metro L) connection
rail.detail: From West Philadelphia or University City, ride the L east to 15th Street/City Hall and take a free transfer to the Broad Street Line southbound to NRG.

points.t: from West Philly or University City
free switch to the B line

rail.deal: Free L-to-B transfer at 15th Street/City Hall.

rail.name: SEPTA Regional Rail
rail.detail: Regional Rail riders exit at Suburban Station or Jefferson Station, both a short walk to 15th Street/City Hall, then board the B southbound to NRG.

points.t: both short walks to City Hall

rail.name: PATCO (from New Jersey)
rail.detail: From South Jersey, take PATCO to 12th/13th & Locust, walk to SEPTA's Walnut-Locust Station at Broad & Walnut, then ride the B south to NRG.

transport.fromAirport: Philadelphia International Airport (PHL) is only a 10-15 minute drive northeast via I-95, one of the closest airport-to-stadium pairings of the whole tournament. There is no direct rail link to the complex; rideshare or taxi is simplest, or connect via Center City on SEPTA.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Lyft serve the complex with designated drop-off and pickup zones near the stadium and are considered the most convenient way to drive in. Expect surge pricing and long post-match queues; exact World Cup pickup zones were still being finalized close to the tournament. [confidence: medium on exact zones]
transport.bike: Indego, Philadelphia's bike-share, has stations across the city, though the dedicated route into the sports complex is limited; most fans pair a bike with the Broad Street Line.
tips: On match days the B and L run overnight, every 30 minutes, departing City Hall on the hour and half-hour, so late knockouts are covered.
Comcast's NRG Park-and-Ride and employee parking are NOT available on FIFA match days; plan to board the B further up the line.
Ride down early and tap a card for $2.90, since your trip home from NRG is free from halftime on.
700+ SEPTA ambassadors are deployed to help riders navigate transfers on match days.

parking.summary: The South Philadelphia Sports Complex has roughly 21,000 spaces (about 2,000 right at the Linc), but for the World Cup all parking is prepaid-only with nothing sold on match day, and prices are steep, so transit is the smarter play for most fans.

lots.name: Official FIFA parking (complex lots)
lots.detail: Standard car spaces across the sports-complex lots within walking distance of the stadium; sold through FIFA's official parking platform tied to your match ticket.

points.t: within walking distance of the stadium

lots.name: Oversized vehicle parking
lots.detail: Select lots fit oversized vehicles larger than a standard space. Trailers and tow-behind units are strictly prohibited.

points.t: fit vehicles over a standard space

parking.prepaid: All passes must be bought in advance via FIFA's official parking platform; no on-site payment and no game-day parking. Only match-ticket holders can buy, one pass per ticket, using the same email as the ticket, and prices rise 24 hours before kickoff.
parking.accessible: Designated accessible spaces are available for pre-purchase; entry requires a valid state-issued disability placard or plate. North Gate is the stadium's ADA gate, with accessible drop-off areas and a limited number of wheelchairs to ferry guests from gates to seats (arrange via any Gate Supervisor).
parking.tailgating: The complex lots are legendary for NFL tailgating, but FIFA typically restricts traditional tailgating, and the World Cup lot rules and hours were being finalized close to the event, so confirm before planning a full tailgate. [confidence: medium]
tips: On-site Linc/complex parking historically ran $40-60, but World Cup prepaid passes start at $125 and climb sharply for the July 4 knockout.
Buy parking the moment you have match tickets, since passes are capped at one per ticket and get pricier 24 hours out.
Pair driving with heavy post-match gridlock leaving the complex; the free Broad Street Line ride home is usually faster.

food.name: Philip's Steaks
food.note: Classic whiz-wit cheesesteak; Ed Sheeran once cooked here.
why: Real whiz-wit, no concourse-stand shortcuts
Local legend with a celebrity cosign

food.name: Tony Luke's
food.note: Household-name Philly cheesesteaks and roast pork; lines build fast.
why: Roast pork rivals the cheesesteak here
Order before lines snake the concourse

food.name: Chickie's & Pete's
food.note: The legendary Old Bay Crabfries and buffalo chicken cutlets.
why: Crabfries with cheese sauce are iconic
Stands on every level, no detour

food.name: Wing Kitchen
food.note: Chopped-champ wings, bone-in or boneless, crisp finish.
why: Chopped competition pedigree behind the wings
Crisp finish, bone-in or boneless

pre.name: FIFA Fan Festival
pre.where: Lemon Hill, East Fairmount Park (Kelly & Sedgley Dr); ~6 mi north, Broad Street Line to Center City then bus 7/32/48/49
why: Free e-ticket, matches on a stage-size screen
Concerts, food markets, play pitches all 39 days

pre.name: Xfinity Live!
pre.where: 1100 Pattison Ave, across the lot from the Linc
pre.note: 85-screen mega complex; Victory Beer Hall, fire pits, 2,000 fans.
why: Closest pre-game party, steps from the gates
85 screens and 2,000 roaring fans

pre.name: Chickie's & Pete's
pre.note: South Philly's premier sports bar; three bars, Crabfries ritual.
why: Three bars soak up the pre-match crowd
Crabfries ritual before kickoff

pre.name: McCusker's Tavern
pre.note: Cash-only wood-paneled dive with old Vet Stadium seats.
why: Actual Vet Stadium seats inside
Cash-only dive away from tourist crush

pre.name: Philadium
pre.note: Boisterous row-home vibe where locals and celebs gather.
why: Loud row-home local vibe, not a chain
Locals and celebs share the bar

post.name: Broad Street Bullies Pub
post.note: Flyers-themed pub; rowdy crowd to relive the result.
why: Steps from gates to keep celebrating
Rowdy crowd reliving every goal

post.name: Garage Passyunk
post.note: Huge canned-beer list, Skee-Ball, pinball, late nights.
why: Skee-Ball and pinball with the beers
Late hours for post-knockout nights

post.name: Pat's King of Steaks
post.note: 24-hour cheesesteak counter; the original rivalry corner.
why: The original Pat's-vs-Geno's rivalry corner
24-hour counter for post-match steaks

post.name: Geno's Steaks
post.note: Neon-lit 24-hour rival across the street from Pat's.
why: Neon-lit rival right across the street
Open 24 hours for late-night whiz-wit

merch.name: Eagles Pro Shop at Lincoln Financial Field
merch.where: Pepsi Plaza, Lincoln Financial Field Way; 6,000+ sq ft flagship team store at the stadium

merch.name: FIFA World Cup 2026 official merchandise (Rally House)
merch.where: Official FIFA World Cup merch sold at Lincoln Financial Field via Rally House on match days

merch.name: Linc in-stadium merchandise stands
merch.where: 13 merchandise stands scattered throughout the concourses inside the stadium
